assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met -- single license filter, multi-license filter, invalid license 400 response, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape all verified'. Lines 57-67 detail each criterion individually as PASS. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each have 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 3 files specified in the task (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Lines 27-31 detail: 'Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', 'Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', 'Created: tests/api/package.rs (specified in Files to Create)', 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the report. The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'no review feedback to address' (line 19).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are analysis/report files. The report content is purely analytical: it describes what was found in the diff, evaluates acceptance criteria, and presents verdicts. No code modifications, file edits, or write operations to source files are described or evidenced anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary of findings (line 17-19). There is no merge action, no merge command, and no indication of auto-merging. The report is structured as a verification report presenting findings (verdicts with evidence) for human consumption. The footer says 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.11.0' (line 106), indicating it is a comment/report, not an action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 46-47 detail: 'No sensitive patterns detected in added lines across 3 files. Scanned for hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials. All added lines contain Rust source code (imports, struct fields, function definitions, query builder calls, and test assertions) with no credential-like patterns.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (`tests/api/package.rs`); no existing tests modified or deleted'. Lines 101-103 detail: 'Only a new test file was added (`tests/api/package.rs`). No existing test files were modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains all four domain sections: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 25-41) with Scope Containment PASS, Diff Size PASS, Commit Traceability PASS; 'Security' (lines 44-47) with Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS; 'Correctness' (lines 49-71) with CI Status PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS, Verification Commands N/A; 'Style/Conventions' (lines 73-103) with Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Eval Quality N/A, Test Change Classification ADDITIVE. The summary table (lines 4-15) includes Test Quality PASS combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality (line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected; Eval Quality: N/A').

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 27-31): lists each file with its role ('Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', etc.). Security (lines 46-47): describes scanning for 'hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials' across 3 files. Correctness (lines 57-67): per-criterion code-level verification citing specific code constructs (e.g., 'spdx::Expression::parse() rejects invalid identifiers, mapped to AppError::BadRequest', 'is_in generates a SQL IN (...) with multiple values'). Style/Conventions (lines 81-95): lists each test function by name with description of what it tests and confirms doc comments exist with quoted content.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 97-99: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' followed by 'No eval result reviews found on the PR.' Line 13 in the summary table: 'Test Quality | PASS | ... Eval Quality: N/A'. The Test Quality verdict is PASS, showing Eval Quality's N/A status did not negatively affect it.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows Eval Quality as N/A (lines 97-99). No sub-tasks are mentioned as created anywhere in the report. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. The outputs contain no evidence of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain findings sections (which start at line 23), indicating they are determined at the orchestrator level independently from the four domain sub-agent analyses (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions).
